reforms.description: The National Recovery and Resilience Plan (NRRP) aims to address structural weaknesses in the Italian economy by providing financial support for public investment, particularly in digital and green transitions, with around 85 billion EUR allocated for the energy and climate transition, focusing on investments in renewable energy, hydrogen development, and energy efficiency improvements in buildings.
reforms.component_name: Public Investment in Energy and Climate
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: The investment in renewable energy and energy efficiency is expected to enhance productivity and support sustainable economic growth.
reforms.status_evidence: The NRRP has been actively implemented since 2021.
reforms.importance_rationale: The NRRP represents a significant structural change in public investment strategy.
reforms.source_quote: Italy’s National Recovery and Resilience Plan (NRRP) features public expenditure worth around 85 billion EUR, or almost 5% of annual GDP, for the energy and climate transition.

reforms.description: The ongoing civil justice reform aims to enhance the efficiency of the judicial system through digitalisation, streamlined procedures, and improved performance incentives for judges, which is expected to support investment and productivity growth.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: Improving judicial efficiency is likely to enhance the business environment and attract investment.
reforms.status_evidence: The reform has been actively implemented since 2023.
reforms.importance_rationale: The reform represents a significant overhaul of the judicial system.
reforms.source_quote: The ongoing civil justice reform constitutes a major step towards a more efficient judicial system.

reforms.description: The competition reform legislated in 2022 aims to reduce entry barriers in services by mandating competitive auctions for public goods, which is expected to enhance competition and productivity in the services sector.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: Enhancing competition is likely to lead to increased efficiency and innovation in the services sector.
reforms.status_evidence: The reform has been actively implemented since 2022.
reforms.importance_rationale: The reform represents a significant change in how public goods are allocated.
reforms.source_quote: The competition reform legislated in 2022 goes some way towards reducing entry barriers in services.

reforms.description: The reform of doctoral programmes aims to align educational paths with current innovation needs and enhance the recruitment of researchers by businesses, thereby improving the relevance of academic training to the labor market.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: Aligning education with innovation needs is expected to improve the quality of the workforce and support economic growth.
reforms.status_evidence: The reform has been announced but not yet implemented.
reforms.importance_rationale: The reform is significant but does not represent a complete overhaul of the system.
reforms.source_quote: The recent reform of doctoral programmes to respond to current innovation needs, promote researchers’ recruitment by businesses and make educational paths increasingly oriented to labour market needs is a further positive step that should be continued.

reforms.description: Italy has approved a tax reform aimed at aligning fossil fuel excise taxes more closely with fossil fuel emissions content, which includes raising excises where they are low compared to emissions content. This reform is intended to reduce emissions and improve the efficiency of carbon pricing.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: Aligning taxes with emissions content is expected to incentivize cleaner technologies and reduce environmental impact.
reforms.status_evidence: The tax reform has been approved and is set to be implemented in 2024.
reforms.importance_rationale: The reform represents a significant change in the taxation system related to environmental policy.
reforms.source_quote: The government recently approved a tax reform to be carried out within the next two years.

reforms.description: The Italian government proposed to restructure its NRRP to include a new REPowerEU Chapter, focusing on simplifying renewable energy permitting and enhancing green skills in the workforce.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: Simplifying permitting processes is expected to accelerate renewable energy projects and enhance workforce skills, supporting economic growth.
reforms.status_evidence: The proposal has been announced but not yet implemented.
reforms.importance_rationale: The restructuring of the NRRP is significant but does not represent a complete overhaul.
reforms.source_quote: The European Commission recently endorsed a proposal by the Italian government to restructure its NRRP and equip it with a new REPowerEU Chapter.

reforms.description: The suitable areas reform aims to streamline the permitting process for renewable energy projects by allowing smaller projects in designated suitable areas to be authorized with lighter procedures. This reform is expected to accelerate the deployment of renewable energy capacity in Italy.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: Streamlining permitting processes is expected to facilitate faster deployment of renewable energy projects, contributing to economic growth.
reforms.status_evidence: The reform has been actively implemented since 2024.
reforms.importance_rationale: The reform is significant but does not represent a complete overhaul of the permitting system.
reforms.source_quote: The suitable areas reform establishes that renewable power plants with capacity below 10 MW to be built in suitable areas can be authorised with the tacit consent principle.

reforms.description: Italy is reforming the tax rebate system for energy efficiency renovations to target the least efficient buildings and complement it with a mix of highly subsidised long-term loans and grants for low-income households. This aims to improve the cost-effectiveness of the renovation schemes and ensure that financial support is directed towards those who need it most.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: Targeting support for energy efficiency renovations is expected to enhance building performance and reduce energy consumption, contributing to economic growth.
reforms.status_evidence: The reform has been announced but not yet implemented.
reforms.importance_rationale: The reform represents a significant change in the approach to energy efficiency renovations.
reforms.source_quote: Italy should reform the tax rebate system to target it to the least efficient buildings and complement it with a system based on a mix of highly subsidised long-term loans and grants for low-income households.

reforms.description: The Italian government is planning a simplification of the legislation regulating the extraction and processing of critical raw materials, which is expected to stimulate innovation by Italian firms in green markets.
reforms.component_name: Simplification of Critical Raw Materials Legislation
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: Simplifying regulations is expected to encourage innovation and investment in critical raw materials.
reforms.status_evidence: The simplification has been announced but not yet implemented.
reforms.importance_rationale: The planned simplification is significant but does not represent a complete overhaul of the regulatory framework.
reforms.source_quote: The government is planning a simplification of the legislation regulating the extraction and processing of critical raw materials.
